articleTitle: Comparison of the efficacy of non-pharmacological interventions for patients with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction: protocol for a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials.
takeaways: This is a protocol for a future systematic review, not a completed study with findings - no specific interventions, dosages, or effectiveness data are available yet
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction affects approximately 50% of all heart failure patients, making it a highly prevalent condition that may benefit from non-drug treatments
The study will focus on patients with left ventricular ejection fraction of 50% or higher who still experience heart failure symptoms despite having relatively normal heart pumping function
Multiple non-pharmacological approaches exist for HFpEF management, but currently there is no scientific consensus on which specific intervention works best for improving symptoms and quality of life
